# Groups Generated by Sets

If we extend the definition of a [[cyclic group]] to include more generators, e.g., $\langle g_{1},g_{2}\rangle$ would create a group the exact same. Formally, we can see that…

Let $G$ be a group and let $S\subseteq  G$. Then the group generated by $S$ is $\langle S\rangle=\{ a_{1}^{n_{1}}a_{2}^{n_{2}} \cdots a_{m}^{n_{m}}:m\in \mathbb{N}\text{ and }n_{i}\in\mathbb{Z}\text{ and }a_{i}\in S\text{ for all } 1\le i \le m\}$. Or, similarly to before, the smallest subgroup of $G$ that contains all elements in $S$.
